results.name: Business Email Compromise - Financial fraud
results.description: Business email compromise (BEC) financial fraud is a social engineering attack that aims to steal money or sensitive information. The attacker tricks the target into believing they are interacting with a trusted entity to conduct either personal or professional business. After deceiving the target, the attacker persuades them to share valuable information or process a payment.

results.name: CIS M365 Foundations Benchmark
results.description: The CIS Microsoft 365 Foundations Benchmark (v3.0.0) is a set of security assessments developed by the Center for Internet Security (CIS). It provides prescriptive guidance for establishing a secure baseline configuration for Microsoft 365. The benchmark includes configuration baselines and best practices for securely configuring a system. The benchmark is internationally recognized as a security standard for defending IT systems and data against cyber attacks. This initiative contains a subset of security assessments recommended by the CIS

results.name: Cloud Security [Preview]
results.description: This initiative aims to reflects the status around cloud security coverage, ROI, health, configuration and performance. It consists of measurements across multiple domains and disciplines to provide security managers with a high-level view into how posture is enforced in cloud ops.

results.name: Critical Asset Protection
results.description: Critical asset protection refers to the strategies, processes, and technologies implemented to safeguard an organization's most valuable and important assets from various threats and risks. It involves identifying, prioritizing, and applying targeted security measures to ensure the resilience and integrity of these critical assets.

suggestions.title: Defender Cloud Security Posture Management plan and agentless VM scanning should be enabled
suggestions.description: To get vulnerability assessment results for cloud resources, go to the Microsoft Defender for Cloud portal, select Environment Settings, choose your subscription, select Defender Plans, and enable the Defender CSPM plan with VM agentless scanning.

suggestions.title: Make sure critical devices are defined correctly
suggestions.description: We recommend using our critical asset management capabilities. We’ve already provided multiple out-of-the-box classifications for critical devices, including domain controllers, ADFS servers, and more. Review your predefined rules to ensure they meet your organization's needs, and set custom classification queries to make sure any device you deem critical is marked accordingly.

actions.text: Learn more on critical asset protection

suggestions.title: Identifying internet facing devices
suggestions.description: We apply multiple methods to identify internet-facing devices that could be exploited by adversaries as entry points to your organization.

suggestions.title: Defender Cloud Security Posture Management plan and agentless containers VA scanning should be enabled
suggestions.description: To get vulnerability assessment results for container images, go to the Microsoft Defender for Cloud portal, choose Environment Settings, select your subscription, select Defender Plans and enable Defender CSPM plan with agentless containers VA scanning configuration.

suggestions.title: Foundational Cloud Security Posture Management plan should be enabled
suggestions.description: To get recommendations for your Azure resources, once you enter the Microsoft Defender for Cloud portal, we will automatically enable the plan for you (free of charge) within a few hours.
